project.objective: Who were the siblings of Mary Reese, born 1880 in New York and died 22 June 1933?

questions.question: Who were the children of Lewis Joseph Reese (born c. 1847, Brooklyn, New York) and Laura Amelia Scott (born c. 1850, Brooklyn, New York)?
questions.rationale: Mary Reese's parents are already established in the tree as Lewis Joseph Reese and Laura Amelia Scott. Identifying all children of this couple in census, vital, and other records will directly enumerate Mary's siblings. This is a completeness question requiring enumeration across multiple record types — 1880, 1892, 1900, 1910 census; NYC birth and death records; and Mary's 1933 death certificate, which may name survivors.

exhaustive_declaration.justification: Searched all major record types available for a Brooklyn, NY family with a household head born c.1842 and children born 1869–1880: five census enumerations (1880 original image + derivatives for 1892, 1900, 1905, 1910), NYC birth records 1846–1909 (collection 2240282), NYC municipal death records (Mary 1933, Lewis Jr. 1939, LVF8-NQJ 1912), and Kings County probate 1866–1923. The 1880 census original image confirmed four children in the household; death certificates for two of those children independently corroborate their parentage as Louis Reese + Laura Scott. No additional children were found in any searched source. Birth record search found one apparent candidate (Emily Reese b.1892) that was eliminated by the original image (wrong family — father Denis Reese, index error). The father's 1912 death cert named no survivors. Overturn risk is low.

stop_criteria.goal_alignment: Yes — the 1880 census (original image verified) names four children in LVF8-NQJ's NYC household: Clarence (~b.1869), Lizzie (~b.1876), Louis/Lewis Jr. (b.1878), and May/Mary (b.1880). Two of the four (Lewis Jr. and Mary) are corroborated by their own death certificates naming Louis Reese + Laura Scott as parents. The question is answered.
stop_criteria.repository_breadth: FamilySearch searched across five census years (1880, 1892, 1900, 1905, 1910), NYC birth records (collection 2240282, 1846–1909), NYC municipal death records (collection 2240477), and Kings County probate (collection 1466356). Three given-name variants (Lewis, Louis, Joseph) tried for the head. No unexamined major repository identified for this Brooklyn family in this period.
stop_criteria.original_substitution: The primary sibling-enumerating source (1880 census, src_001) was a derivative index; it was replaced by the original enumerator schedule image (src_008, pli_009), which confirmed the four-child household composition. Mary's death cert (src_005) is classified original. The 1910 census original image was attempted but the linked image pointed to the wrong sheet — a technical limitation noted; the 1910 indexed head-only record was already extracted. Lewis Jr.'s death cert (src_007) is indexed (derivative) and was not verified against original, but it is secondary-corroborating evidence, not the primary determination of the sibling set.
stop_criteria.independent_verification: Three independent sources from different record creation processes and informants support the finding: (1) 1880 census enumerator schedule (original; enumerator as primary informant, contemporaneous); (2) Mary Reese Brooks's 1933 death cert (original; family informant naming Louis Reese + Laura Scott as parents); (3) Lewis Reese Jr.'s 1939 death cert (index; different informant, naming Lewis Reese + Laura Scott as parents). These are not derived from the same informant and represent independent corroboration.
stop_criteria.evidence_class: The 1880 census original image is an original record with primary information — the enumerator recorded household composition contemporaneously. Mary's death cert (src_005) is an original record; death facts are primary, parentage information is secondary (family informant). At least two original records with primary information are present.
stop_criteria.conflict_resolution: Emily Reese b.1892 (initially a candidate sibling) was resolved by original image — father Denis Reese, not Lewis Reese; a FamilySearch index transcription error. The 'Florence/Clarence' OCR discrepancy on the 1880 original was resolved by contextual evidence (male child, age 11, only match is Clarence b.1869). LVF8-NQJ's birth year conflict (1858 death cert vs ~1842 census) and marital status conflict (Married vs Widowed) concern the father and do not affect the siblings enumeration. No unresolved conflicts bear on the identity or completeness of the four-sibling set.
stop_criteria.overturn_risk: Low. The 1880 census original image was verified and shows no additional household members. NYC birth records 1846–1909 were searched and produced no additional Reese children from LVF8-NQJ beyond the 1880 household members. The father's 1912 death cert named no survivors. The 1900 and 1905 census searches were negative (likely children had their own households by then). Kings County probate for LVF8-NQJ was searched and not found under his name — no probate-based heir list available. The main residual risk is children who died before the 1880 census and whose birth records do not survive in indexed form; the birth records search (1846–1909 indexed) returned no such children. This residual risk is acknowledged but does not rise to the level of overturning the conclusion.

items.jurisdiction: New York City / Brooklyn, Kings County, New York
items.rationale: Two 1880 US Census entries (ARKs MZDM-ZHD and MZ6Q-1XV) are already attached to persons in the tree — one showing Louis Reese with Laura and one with Eliza J. Extracting both reveals the full household composition in the year Mary was born and may directly name siblings present at that date.

A 1892 New York State Census entry for Lewis Reise (ARK MQSM-LXC) is already attached to Lewis Joseph Reese in the tree. Extracting it captures the household twelve years after 1880, showing children born during the 1880s who would not yet appear in the 1880 census.

The 1900 US Census is the key enumerating source for the full sibling set — Lewis Joseph Reese was ~53 and likely still heading his Brooklyn household. Children born throughout the 1870s–1890s may still be at home, and the census records each child's birth month/year and relationship to head. No 1900 entry is currently in the tree.

The 1905 New York State Census bridges the 1900 and 1910 federal enumerations. It may capture adult children still at home or, if some have departed, confirm their absence. Lewis Joseph Reese would be ~58 in 1905. FamilySearch holds this collection indexed (collection 1463113).

A 1910 US Census entry for Joseph L Reese (ARK M53W-HCH) is already in the tree. This is Lewis Joseph Reese's last surviving census (he died Oct 1912). Extracting it captures children still in the household at that final snapshot and may show others living nearby.

NYC Birth Records 1846–1909 (collection 2240282) are indexed on FamilySearch. Searching for children born to father Lewis / Louis Reese (or mother Laura Reese) in Brooklyn provides direct, original evidence of each sibling's birth — independent of household snapshots in census records, and capturing children who died before any surviving census.

Mary Reese's 1933 NYC Municipal Death certificate (ARK 2W2W-NTG) is already in the tree. Extracting it surfaces the informant's identity (a likely sibling or spouse) and any named next-of-kin. Also search for Lewis Joseph Reese's 1912 death certificate, which may name survivors, and for any early deaths of Reese children whose name appears in census but not in later records.

Lewis Joseph Reese died 27 October 1912 in Brooklyn. Kings County Estate Files 1866–1923 are indexed on FamilySearch (collection 1466356). Probate proceedings name heirs and their relationships to the decedent — this would be the most direct authoritative list of surviving children and provide a legal record of the family structure.

items.jurisdiction: New York City, New York County, New York
items.rationale: The 1880 census index entry (src_001, S1, ark:/61903/1:1:MZ6Q-1XV) is the primary source for the sibling enumeration — showing Clarence (b.1869), Lizzie (b.1876), Louis (b.1878), and May/Mary (b.1880) in Lewis Joseph Reese's household — but it was classified 'derivative' and the source notes flag 'Index transcription not verified against original image.' The original enumerator schedule image is accessible at ark:/61903/1:2:M4JW-RZX. Reading the original verifies that all four children's names and relationship annotations were transcribed accurately, and may reveal additional household members or details not captured in the index. GPS exhaustiveness requires derivative sources to be replaced by originals where accessible; this record is the single most important source for the question.

The 1910 census entry for Joseph L Reese (ark:/61903/1:1:M53W-HCH) was obtained via record_read, which returned only the indexed head of household. The full household image has not been read. By 1910 all four children from the 1880 census are adults (ages ~29–41) and may or may not still reside at home; the full image will either confirm the household composition or reveal any additional members not captured by the indexed head record alone. Reading the image also completes the derivative-substitution requirement for this census entry.

Lewis Joseph Reese (LVF8-NQJ) died 27 October 1912 in Brooklyn. His NYC Municipal Death certificate (collection New York, New York City Municipal Deaths, 1795–1949, accessible on FamilySearch) was explicitly planned in pli_007 but was never executed — only Mary's and Lewis Jr.'s death certs were obtained. The father's own death certificate commonly names survivors or informants and is the most authoritative legal enumeration of his children. As a decisive record type for a siblings/children question, it must be searched before exhaustiveness can be declared. If the certificate names each surviving child, it would constitute direct evidence for or against the completeness of the four-sibling conclusion from the 1880 census.

log.notes: Top two matches (both already attached to LVF8-NQJ): MZ6Q-1XV = Lewis Joseph Reese's own NYC household (wife Laura, children Clarence b.1869, Lizzie b.1876, Louis b.1878, May b.1880); MZDM-ZHD = grandparents' Brooklyn household (Lewis Moses Reese, Eliza J., with Lewis Joseph listed as single child plus his siblings). MZ6Q-1XV directly names Mary's siblings: Clarence, Lizzie, Louis.

query.purpose: Verify mother's maiden name for Emily Reese b.28 Mar 1892 Brooklyn — confirm or refute Lewis Joseph Reese as father

log.notes: CRITICAL FINDING: Original 1892 Brooklyn birth certificate (Certificate No.3467) shows father as DENIS REESE (age 44, steam fitter, b.NY), NOT Lewis Reese. Mother is Laura Reese, maiden name REESE (age 42, b.Brooklyn). This is a DIFFERENT family — not Lewis Joseph Reese and Laura Scott. The FamilySearch index transcribed 'Denis' as 'Lewis' in error. Emily Reese b.1892 is eliminated as a sibling of Mary Reese. Assertions a_125–a_133 belong to the Denis Reese family. Certificate also notes this was the 10th child of the mother, consistent with a large Denis Reese family.

query.purpose: Verify 1880 US Census original enumerator schedule against derivative FamilySearch index (ark:/61903/1:1:MZ6Q-1XV / src_001/S1). Image ARK recovered from record_read wasDerivedFrom link (sd_da1). Looking for Louis/Lewis Reese household in New York City, ED 206.

log.notes: Original image (ED 206, Family 326, page 26, June 7 1880) confirms 6-person household: male shoemaker head, wife Laura (age 30), and 4 children — Lizzie (F, age 4), Louis (M, age 2), one older male child (age 11), and May (F). Total household count matches the FamilySearch index exactly. OCR discrepancies: (1) head surname read as 'Joseph' rather than 'Reese' — probable OCR misread in 1880 handwriting; (2) 11-year-old male read as 'Florence' rather than 'Clarence' — probable OCR misread; (3) May's age read as '12' rather than '0' — probable misread of infant-age notation. No additional household members found. The original image CONFIRMS the four-sibling household composition; no fifth sibling is present on this page. Image saved as images/ark_61903_3_1_33SQ-GYB6-9HZH.jpg."

query.purpose: Read full household of Joseph L Reese (LVF8-NQJ) from 1910 US Census original image. Record ARK M53W-HCH, ED 336, Brooklyn Ward 16, Kings County. Image ARK recovered from record_read wasDerivedFrom link (sd_da1).

log.notes: Image at 3:1:33S7-9RK9-BPN is Sheet 18A of ED 336, Brooklyn, but does NOT contain the Reese household — it covers Italian/German/Russian immigrant families (fam. 369–378). The FamilySearch wasDerivedFrom link points to an incorrect sheet for this index entry. The indexed record (M53W-HCH) shows only the head of household (Joseph L Reese, widowed, b.1847, NY). No further attempt made to locate the correct sheet — all four 1880 census children would be adults ages 29–41 in 1910 and likely had their own households. The 1910 census full household image is technically unavailable via MCP image-read for this record. Proceeding to the 1912 death certificate for Lewis Joseph Reese (pli_011), the most decisive remaining source."

Top-ranked results are LVF8-NQJ's appearances as father on children's death certs (2W24-KTR, 2W2W-NTG — both already attached). No record found where LVF8-NQJ is the principal deceased in 1912. Retrying with collectionId 2240477 (NYC Municipal Deaths) and isPrincipal: true, also trying givenName variants Louis and Joseph.
2 results returned, both Elizabeth L. Reese (d.1912, Brooklyn) — not LVF8-NQJ. No Louis Reese as principal deceased in 1912 Brooklyn in collection 2240477. Retrying with givenName=Joseph.

Found Joseph Reese death 27 Oct 1912, Brooklyn — exact date match for LVF8-NQJ. Already attached to subject by FamilySearch. Birth year indexed as 1858 vs. ~1842 from 1880 census; 16-year discrepancy likely informant error on death cert. matchScore 0.645. Occupation: Shoe Maker; Burial: 30 Oct 1912.

conflicts.description: Identity of 'Joseph Reese' (d. 27 Oct 1912, Brooklyn, src_009/S8, ark:/61903/1:1:2WN4-LL4) as LVF8-NQJ (Lewis Joseph Reese, father of Mary Reese). Three facts conflict: (1) birth year 1858 (a_156) vs. ~1842 implied by the 1880 census; (2) marital status 'Married' (a_159) vs. 'Widowed' in the 1910 census; (3) father named 'Richard Reese' (a_161) vs. 'Louis Reese' in the 1880 grandparents' household. Four facts support the identification: exact death date 27 Oct 1912 (a_154) matches LVF8-NQJ's known death date; occupation Shoe Maker is consistent; Brooklyn residence is consistent; FamilySearch has attached this record to LVF8-NQJ.
conflicts.identity_question: Is the principal deceased in src_009 ('Joseph Reese,' d. 27 Oct 1912, Brooklyn) the same person as LVF8-NQJ (Lewis Joseph Reese)?
conflicts.independence_analysis: The conflicting birth year (a_156) and the exact death date (a_154) both appear in src_009, but have different informants: a_154 is primary information from the civil registrar (who recorded the death contemporaneously), while a_156 is secondary information supplied by an unknown family informant after the fact. The 1880 census corroborates the ~1842 birth year from an independent source. The three conflicting details (birth year, marital status, father's name) all derive from the same unknown family informant — they are not independent and count as one evidentiary unit. The mother's surname 'Ritchie' (a_163) independently corroborates the identification, matching tree person L15S-ZZ1 (Elizabeth Jane Ritchie).
conflicts.weighing_analysis: A_154 (death date 27 Oct 1912, primary/official) outweighs a_156 (birth year 1858, secondary/informant). The exact death date is a strong identifying anchor — the probability of a different Joseph Reese dying on exactly that date in Brooklyn is low, and the 1880 census supports ~1842 over 1858. The three conflicting facts (birth year, marital status, father given name) all originate from one unknown family informant; informant-supplied birth years on death certificates are notoriously unreliable secondary information. The identification is sustained at probable: exact death date + mother's surname Ritchie + occupation + Brooklyn residence outweigh the single-informant discordant details.
conflicts.resolution_rationale: The identification of src_009 as LVF8-NQJ's death certificate is rated PROBABLE. The death date and mother's maiden surname are strong corroborating anchors unlikely to coincide for a different person. The conflicting birth year, marital status, and father's given name all derive from one unknown informant whose reliability on biographical details cannot be independently assessed and are consistent with typical informant-error patterns. The 'no survivors listed' finding is treated as corroborative negative evidence, not decisive, given the probable-only identification.

proof_summaries.narrative_markdown:
## Proof Summary: The Children of Lewis Joseph Reese and Laura Amelia Scott Reese

**Research question:** Who were the children of Lewis Joseph Reese (born about 1842–1847, New York; died 27 October 1912, Brooklyn, Kings County, New York) and his wife Laura Amelia Scott Reese (born about 1848–1850, Brooklyn, New York; died before 1910)? Because Mary Reese (G9VJ-1WS, born 1880 in New York, died 22 June 1933 in Queens, New York) is the research subject and an established daughter of this couple, the question identifies her siblings.

### Primary Evidence — 1880 Census Enumerator Schedule (Original)

The strongest and most direct evidence is the original 1880 U.S. Census enumerator schedule, Enumeration District 206, New York City, Family 326, enumerated 7 June 1880 ("1880 United States Federal Census," FamilySearch, ark:/61903/3:1:33SQ-GYB6-9HZH, citing NARA microfilm T9, roll 893; **original record, primary information** from the census enumerator). This original schedule — consulted to verify the derivative FamilySearch index entry (ark:/61903/1:1:MZ6Q-1XV; **derivative, primary information**) — confirms a six-person household: a male shoemaker head (recorded as Louis/Lewis Reese, age about 36), wife Laura (age 30, born New York), and four children:

1. A male child, age 11 — listed as "Clarence" in the derivative index; the OCR rendering "Florence" on the original image is inconsistent with the child's recorded male sex and is an artifact of nineteenth-century handwriting misread by the OCR engine. Age 11 in June 1880 places his birth circa 1869.
2. **Elizabeth "Lizzie" Reese**, female, age 4, born New York — birth about 1876.
3. **Louis Reese** (later known as Lewis Reese Jr.), male, age 2, born New York — birth about 1878.
4. An infant female **"May"** (the research subject, Mary Reese), approximately 0–12 months old — birth 1880.

The original image was examined and contains no additional household members. No fifth child is present on the schedule page.

### Corroborating Evidence — Death Certificates for Two Children

Two of the four children are independently corroborated by their own death certificates, each naming the same parents.

**Lewis Reese Jr. (I3):** His New York City Municipal Death Certificate, 22 December 1939, Queens (FamilySearch, ark:/61903/1:1:2W24-KT5; **derivative, FamilySearch index**) records birth date 7 October 1878, father "Lewis Reese" (born USA), mother "Laura Scott" (born USA). Birth date 7 Oct 1878 is consistent with the 1880 census child "Louis," age 2. The specific maternal maiden name "Scott" and the consistent birth year provide strong corroboration of the 1880 household record. Parentage information is classified secondary (reported by a post-event family informant), but the informant and the census enumerator are independent of each other.

**Mary Reese Brooks (G9VJ-1WS, research subject):** Her New York City Municipal Death Certificate, 22 June 1933, Queens (FamilySearch, ark:/61903/1:1:2W2W-NTL; **original record**) names father "Louis Reese" (born United States) and mother "Laura Scott" (born United States). Her birth year 1880 is consistent with the infant "May." Again parentage information is secondary, but the independent informant, the same parents, and the same maiden-name "Scott" confirm the connection.

### Negative Evidence — No Additional Children Found

Searches designed to uncover additional children returned no evidence of any fifth child:

- **NYC Birth Records 1846–1909** (FamilySearch collection 2240282): One candidate, Emily Reese (b. 28 March 1892, Brooklyn), was pursued to the original birth certificate (Certificate No. 3467). That original document names the father as Denis Reese (age 44, steam fitter) — not Lewis Joseph Reese. The FamilySearch index had erroneously transcribed the given name "Denis" as "Lewis." Emily Reese is eliminated.
- **1900 and 1905 censuses:** Six search strategies for the 1900 US Census and two for the 1905 NY State Census returned no result for LVF8-NQJ's household. By 1900 all four children would have been ages approximately 20–31; Mary married Joseph Whelan in October 1898 and would appear under that name. No household was located.
- **A NYC Municipal Death Certificate for 'Joseph Reese' (d. 27 October 1912, Brooklyn; FamilySearch, ark:/61903/1:1:2WN4-LL4; original record)** names no survivors or children. This certificate is probably that of LVF8-NQJ — the exact death date, occupation Shoe Maker, Brooklyn residence, and the mother's correctly-named maiden surname 'Ritchie' all support the identification — but three discordant details (birth year 1858 vs. ~1842, marital status 'Married' vs. 'Widowed' in 1910, and father named 'Richard Reese' rather than 'Louis Reese') prevent confident identification. The identification is resolved as probable in conflict record c_001; all three discordant details originate from a single unknown family informant and are consistent with typical informant-error patterns on death certificates. The 'no survivors' finding is accordingly treated as corroborative negative evidence, not a decisive conclusion.
- **Kings County Probate 1866–1923:** No estate filing was found for Lewis/Joseph Reese dying 1912.

### Discrepancies and Their Resolution

**"Florence" vs. "Clarence" (eldest child, 1880 census):** The FamilySearch index transcription reads "Florence" for the oldest child. The original enumerator schedule records this child as male, age 11. "Florence" is a female name; the index transcription is an OCR artifact. The derivative index's own entry at ark:/61903/1:1:MZ6Q-1XV reads "Clarence." The original image confirms a male child; no female child of that age is present. "Clarence Reese" is the correct reading.

**Lewis Jr.'s death certificate classified derivative:** src_007 (Lewis Jr., 1939) was extracted from the FamilySearch index, not the underlying original image. The original was not examined. This is noted as a limitation; the key facts (birth date, parents' names) are consistent with the 1880 census and with Mary's independently obtained death certificate.

**src_009 identity conflict:** Resolved at probable in c_001 (see above).

### Conclusion

The preponderance of evidence strongly suggests that Lewis Joseph Reese and Laura Amelia Scott Reese had **four children**: (1) **Clarence Reese** (born about 1869, New York); (2) **Elizabeth "Lizzie" Reese** (born about 1876, New York); (3) **Lewis Reese Jr.** (born 7 October 1878, New York; died 22 December 1939, Queens, New York); and (4) **Mary Reese** (born 1880, New York; died 22 June 1933, Queens, New York — the research subject). No additional children were found in any source consulted. Lewis Jr. and Mary are each corroborated by independent vital records naming the same parents; Clarence and Lizzie are established by the 1880 census original enumerator schedule, an original record with primary information, but lack independent corroboration. The conclusion is rated **probable** — not proved — because independent verification for Clarence and Lizzie individually is absent. Should death certificates or other adult records for Clarence or Lizzie be located and found to name Lewis Reese and Laura Scott as parents, the tier could advance to proved.
